transcript.text: IŌĆÖve got enough context to switch from discovery to implementation. The app is a single-page Wikipedia feed with one provider, one root stylesheet, `localStorage`-backed likes/language state, fetch-driven article loading, fixed overlays rendered inline, and interaction-time use of share/clipboard/reload rather than any custom portal roots.

IŌĆÖm at the point of changing the setup now. First IŌĆÖm adding the missing Storybook test dependencies the repo doesnŌĆÖt currently have, then IŌĆÖll wire `.storybook/preview.tsx` so stories inherit the real provider tree, CSS, seeded browser state, and shared MSW handlers instead of carrying their own setup.

The dependency install is in flight. As soon as that settles, IŌĆÖm going to replace the stock Storybook scaffold and centralize the real app runtime there so the stories stay small and pattern-based.

content.text: IŌĆÖve got enough context to switch from discovery to implementation. The app is a single-page Wikipedia feed with one provider, one root stylesheet, `localStorage`-backed likes/language state, fetch-driven article loading, fixed overlays rendered inline, and interaction-time use of share/clipboard/reload rather than any custom portal roots.

IŌĆÖm at the point of changing the setup now. First IŌĆÖm adding the missing Storybook test dependencies the repo doesnŌĆÖt currently have, then IŌĆÖll wire `.storybook/preview.tsx` so stories inherit the real provider tree, CSS, seeded browser state, and shared MSW handlers instead of carrying their own setup.

The dependency install is in flight. As soon as that settles, IŌĆÖm going to replace the stock Storybook scaffold and centralize the real app runtime there so the stories stay small and pattern-based.

content.text:
Storybook now mirrors the app runtime without changing app code. The shared setup in [.storybook/preview.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T14-42-01Z-b2062380/project/frontend/.storybook/preview.tsx) imports [src/index.css](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T14-42-01Z-b2062380/project/frontend/src/index.css), wraps stories in the real [LikedArticlesProvider](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T14-42-01Z-b2062380/project/frontend/src/contexts/LikedArticlesContext.tsx), seeds the `lang` and `likedArticles` browser state the app actually reads, freezes time with `mockdate`, and registers global MSW handlers from [.storybook/msw-handlers.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T14-42-01Z-b2062380/project/frontend/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts) using shared article fixtures in [.storybook/wiki-fixtures.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T14-42-01Z-b2062380/project/frontend/.storybook/wiki-fixtures.ts). I also added [public/mockServiceWorker.js](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T14-42-01Z-b2062380/project/frontend/public/mockServiceWorker.js), enabled `public` as a Storybook static dir in [.storybook/main.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T14-42-01Z-b2062380/project/frontend/.storybook/main.ts), and included `.storybook` files in [tsconfig.node.json](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T14-42-01Z-b2062380/project/frontend/tsconfig.node.json). I didnŌĆÖt add `preview-body.html` because the app doesnŌĆÖt use portals.

The scaffold stories were removed and replaced with real app-pattern stories in [src/App.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T14-42-01Z-b2062380/project/frontend/src/App.stories.tsx), [src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T14-42-01Z-b2062380/project/frontend/src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx), and [src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T14-42-01Z-b2062380/project/frontend/src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx). Every named export has a `play` function, and those plays verify real rendering and behavior: mocked Wikipedia data loads, provider-backed liked state appears, overlays open, search filters liked articles, dropdowns open/close, and wiki cards can be liked through the real context.

Verification is clean:
`npx tsc --noEmit`
`STORYBOOK_FAIL_ON_EMPTY_RENDER=true npx vitest --project storybook src/App.stories.tsx src/components/WikiCard.stories.tsx src/components/LanguageSelector.stories.tsx`

That final Vitest run passed `10/10` tests. The only unrelated file still uncommitted is [.storybook/eval-results/prompt.md](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T14-42-01Z-b2062380/project/frontend/.storybook/eval-results/prompt.md).
